elements.sent-2411-001: In this article we obtain the characterization for the commutators of maximal functions on the weighted Morrey spaces in the setting of spaces of homogeneous type.
elements.sent-2411-002: More precisely, we characterize BMO spaces using the commutators of Hardy-Littlewood maximal function, sharp and fractional maximal functions.
elements.section-2411-001: \bf Introduction and Statement of Main Results
elements.sent-2411-003: In 2000, Bastero, Milman and Ruiz \cite{jmf studied the necessary and sufficient condition for the boundedness of commutator of the Hardy–Littlewood maximal function and the commutator of the sharp maximal function on $L^p(\mathbb{R ^n)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-004: Later, Zhang and Wu \cite{zlw , obtained a similar result for the commutator of the fractional maximal function.}
elements.sent-2411-005: Since then, the boundedness of these classical operators and their commutators have been extensively studied on various function spaces in the Euclidean setting, such as the weighted $L^p$ spaces (e.g. \cite{dca, dsa ) and Morrey spaces (e.g. \cite{a, ss, bg1, bg2, gcm ).}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Motivated by these results, in this article we provide the boundedness characterizations for maximal function commutators on the Morrey spaces (weighted) in the general setting of spaces of homogeneous type.
elements.sent-2411-007: In modern harmonic analysis, a central focus has been extending the real-variable theory beyond the Euclidean framework, where the underlying space is $\mathbb{R ^n$, equipped with the Euclidean metric and Lebesgue measure, to more general settings such as the spaces of homoegenous type.}
elements.sent-2411-008: These spaces have been extensive studied; see, for e.g., \cite{clw, nvm2, rg1, rg2, vnm1, djs, dh, cmlv, cfiv .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Consider $(X,d,\mu)$, a space of homogeneous type and let $p\in (1,\infty)$ and $\kappa\in (0,1)$.
elements.sent-2411-010:

u(B)]^\kappa}\int_{B}|f(h)|^p \omega(h)d\mu(h)ight\}^ rac{1}{p}.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: If $\omega = \nu,$ then we denote by $L_{\omega ^{p,\kappa (X)$, the classical weighted Morrey space.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: Note that, when $\kappa=0,$ then $L_{\omega, \nu ^{p,\kappa (X)= L^p(X,\omega),$ the weighted $L^p$ space on $X.$ Morrey spaces, alongside weighted $L^p(w)$ spaces, play a pivotal role in the study of partial differential equations.}}
elements.sent-2411-013: In particular, Morrey spaces have proven highly useful for studying the local behavior of solutions to elliptic partial differential equations and have been heavily researched (see, for example \cite{gvqx,jm, akl, rjem, ssht, vsg ).}
elements.sent-2411-014: Since the weighted Morrey spaces naturally generalize weighted Lebesgue spaces, thus, along this literature, it is natural to study the boundedness of maximal operators commutators on these spaces.
elements.sent-2411-015: We now recall the definition of the the Hardy--Littlewood maximal function on the space of homogeneous type $(X,d,\mu)$.
elements.sent-2411-016: For a locally integrable function $f$ on $X$, the Hardy--Littlewood maximal function $ M_pf(x)$, $1\leq p<\infty$ is defined as \begin{equation}M_pf(x):=\sup_{B \ni x} \left({\frac{1}{\mu(B)}}\int_B |f(y)|^p\,d\mu(y)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}},\end{equation} where the supremum is taken over all balls $B\subset X$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Observe that, for $p=1$, $M=M_1$ is the classical Hardy–Littlewood maximal operator.
elements.sent-2411-018: For a fixed ball $B_0 \in X$, we denote by $M_{p, B_0 f(x)$, the Hardy–Littlewood maximal function relative to $B_0$, \begin{equation}M_{p, B_0}f(x):=\sup_{x\in B \subset B_0} \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B |f(y)|^p\,d\mu(y)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}},\end{equation} Now we recall that, the commutator of the Hardy–Littlewood maximal operator given $b\in L^1_{loc (X)$, is defined for all $x\in X$ as \begin{equation}[M_p, b]f(x) := b(x)M_pf(x) - M_P(bf)(x).\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-019: Below, we state our result concerning the boundedness characterization for the commutator of the Hardy--Littlewood maximal functions.
elements.sent-2411-020: Let $\kappa\in(0,1), \omega\in A_p(X)$ for $1<p<\infty$ and $b$ be a real valued, locally integrable function in $(X,d,\mu).$ Then the following conditions are equivalent:.
elements.sent-2411-022: The commutator $[M_p, b]$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X)$, $p< q < \infty$.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: For some $q\in (1,\infty),$ there exists some constant $C$, such that \begin{equation}\sup_{B} \frac{\|(b - M_{p,B}(b))\chi_B\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_B\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}} < C.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-024: For a locally integrable function $b(x)$, we denote by $C_b$, the maximal commutator on $X$, \begin{equation}C_b(f)(x):=\sup_{B\ni x}{\frac{1}{\mu(B)}}\int_{B}|b(x)-b(y)| |f(y)|d\mu(y),\end{equation} where the supremum is taken over all balls $B\subset X$.
elements.sent-2411-025: Note that, from \cite{mgkm , we have the following upperbound for the maximal commutator $C_b$ \begin{equation}\label{maxcommu} C_b(f)(x) \leq C\|b\|_{BMO(X)}M^2(f)(x).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-026: As a consequence of the Theorem \ref{thm main1 and the equation \ref{maxcommu we obtain the following boundedness characterization for the maximal commutator.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Let $b$ be a locally integrable function on $(X,d,\mu)$, and $1<p<\infty$ and $\omega \in A_p(X)$.
elements.sent-2411-028: Then the maximal commutator $C_b$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{p,\kappa (X)$ if and only if $b\in BMO(X).$.}}
elements.sent-2411-029: For any $f\in L^1_{loc (X)$ and $x\in X,$ the sharp maximal function of Fefferman and Stein, $M^{\sharp f$ is given as \begin{equation}M^{\sharp}f(x) = \sup_{B \ni x} {1\over \mu(B)}\int_B |f(y) - f_B|\,d\mu(y),\end{equation} where $f_B = \frac{1 {\mu(B)}\int_{B f(y) du(y)$, the average value of $f$ on $B$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: And for some $\gamma \in (0,1)$, we define the fractional maximal function $M_{\gamma}$ for $f\in L^1_{loc (X)$ and $x\in X,$ as \begin{equation}M_{\gamma}f(x):=\sup_{B \ni x} \frac{1}{\mu(B)^{1-\gamma}}\int_B |f(y)|\,d\mu(y).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-031: For any fixed ball $B_0 \in X$, we will denote by $M_{\gamma, B_0 f(x)$, the fractional maximal function relative to $B_0$.}
elements.sent-2411-032: Now, we state our boundedness results for the commutators of sharp maximal functions and fractional maximal functions.
elements.sent-2411-033: Let $\kappa\in(0,1), \omega\in A_{1 (X)$ and $b$ be a real valued, locally integrable function in $(X,d,\mu).$ Then the following conditions are equivalent:.}
elements.sent-2411-034: The commutator $[ M^{\sharp , b]$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X)$, $1< q < \infty$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: For some $q\in (1,\infty),$ there exists some constant $C$, such that \begin{equation}\sup_{B}\frac{\|(b - 2M^{\sharp}(b))\chi_B\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_B\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}} < C.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-037: Then for $b$ be a real valued, locally integrable function in $(X,d,\mu)$ the following conditions are equivalent:.
elements.sent-2411-038: The commutator $[M_{\gamma , b]$ is bounded from $L_{(\omega^p, \omega^q) ^{p,\kappa (X)$ to $L_{\omega^q ^{q,\frac{\kappa q {p (X)$.}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-040: This paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-041: In Section \ref{s2 , we will give some preliminary definitions and recall some results on space of homogeneous type.}
elements.sent-2411-042: In section 3 and 4 we describe the proofs of our boundedness results.
elements.sent-2411-043: Throughout this paper, we denote by $C$ and $\tilde{C}$, some positive constants which are independent of main parameters and they may vary from line to line.
elements.section-2411-002: \bf Preliminaries on Spaces of Homogeneous Type
elements.sent-2411-044: We say that $(X,d,\mu)$ is a {space of homogeneous type in the sense of Coifman and Weiss (\cite{rg1 ) if $d$ is a quasi-metric on~$X$ and $\mu$ is a nonzero measure satisfying the doubling condition.}}
elements.sent-2411-045: A quasi-metric~$d$ on a set~$X$ is a function $d: X\times X\longrightarrow[0,\infty)$ satisfying (i) $d(x,y) = d(y,x) \geq 0$ for all $x$, $y\in X$; (ii) $d(x,y) = 0$ if and only if $x = y$; and (iii) the quasi-triangle inequality: there is a constant $A_0\in [1,\infty)$ such that for all $x$, $y$, $z\in X$, \begin{eqnarray}\label{eqn:quasitriangleineq} d(x,y) \leq A_0 [d(x,z) + d(z,y)].\end{eqnarray}.
elements.sent-2411-046: We say that a nonzero measure $\mu$ satisfies the doubling condition if there is a constant $C_\mu$ such that for all $x\in X$ and $r > 0$, \begin{eqnarray}\label{doubling condition} \mu(B(x,2r)) \leq C_\mu \mu(B(x,r)) < \infty,\end{eqnarray} where $B(x,r)$ is the quasi-metric ball given by $B(x,r) := \{y\in X: d(x,y) < r\}$ for $x\in X$ and $r > 0$.We point out that the doubling condition (\ref{doubling condition ) implies that there exists a positive constant $n$ (the upper dimension of~$\mu$) such that for all $x\in X$, $\lambda\geq 1$ and $r > 0$, \begin{eqnarray}\label{upper dimension} \mu(B(x, \lambda r)) \leq C_\mu\lambda^{n} \mu(B(x,r)).\end{eqnarray}.}
elements.sent-2411-047: A subset $\Omega\subseteq X$ is open (in the topology induced by $\rho$) if for every $x\in\Omega$ there exists $\eps>0$ such that $B(x,\eps)\subseteq\Omega$.
elements.sent-2411-048: A subset $F\subseteq X$ is closed if its complement $X\setminus F$ is open.
elements.sent-2411-049: The usual proof of the fact that $F\subseteq X$ is closed, if and only if it contains its limit points, carries over to the quasi-metric spaces.
elements.sent-2411-050: Throughout this paper we assume that $\mu(X) = \infty$ and that $\mu(x_0) = 0$ for every $x_0 \in X$.
elements.sent-2411-051: Let $\omega(x)$ be a nonnegative locally integrable function on~$X$.
elements.sent-2411-052: For $1 < p < \infty$, we say $\omega$ is an $A_p(X)$ weight, written $\omega\in A_p$, if \begin{equation}[\omega]_{A_p} := \sup_B \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B \omega(x)d\mu(x)\right) \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B \omega(x)^{1-p'}d\mu(x)\right)^{p-1} < \infty.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-053: Here the suprema are taken over all balls~$B\subset X$.
elements.sent-2411-054: The quantity $[\omega]_{A_p}$ is called the $A_p$~constant of~$\omega$.
elements.sent-2411-055: For $p = 1$, we say $\omega$ is an $A_1(X)$ weight, written $\omega\in A_1$, if $M(\omega)(x)\leq \omega(x)$ for $\mu$-almost every $x\in X$ and we define $A_{\infty (X) = \cup_{1<p<\infty A_p(X)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-057: We say a weight function $\omega$ belongs to $A_{p,q}$, for $1<p<q<\infty,$ if there exists a constant $C>1$ such that \begin{equation}[\omega]_{A_{p,q}} := \sup_B \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B \omega(x)^q d\mu(x)\right)^{1/q} \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B \omega(x)^{-p'}d\mu(x)\right)^{1/p'} < \infty\end{equation} where $1/p+1/p'=1$.
elements.sent-2411-058: When $p=1$, $\omega\in A_{1,q}$ with $1<q<\infty$ if there exists $C>1$ such that \begin{equation}[\omega]_{A_{p,q}} := \sup_B \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B \omega(x)^q d\mu(x)\right) \left(ess sup_{x\in B}\frac{1}{\omega(x)}\right)^{1/p'} < \infty\end{equation} We have the following characterization for the weights.
elements.sent-2411-059: Next we recall the definition of the weighted BMO space on space of homogeneous type, while we point out that the Euclidean version was first introduced by Muckenhoupt and Wheeden \cite{brr .}
elements.sent-2411-060: A function $b\in L^1_{\rm loc (X)$ belongs to the BMO space $BMO(X)$ if \begin{equation}\|b\|_{BMO(X)}:=\sup_{B}{\frac{1}{\mu(B)}}\int_{B} \left|b(x)-b_{B}\right|\, d\mu(x)<\infty,\end{equation} where the sup is taken over all quasi-metric balls $B\subset X$ and \begin{equation}b_B= {1\over\mu(B)} \int_B f(y)d\mu(y).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-061: Note that we have the following equivalent norm for the BMO(X) functions, \begin{equation}\|b\|_{BMO(X)} \sim \frac{1}{\mu(B)}\inf_{c\in \mathbb{R}}\int_B|b(t)-c|d\mu(t)\end{equation} Below we list useful remarks and lemma's required in the proof of our main results.
elements.sent-2411-062: We use this to prove the following lemma that gives the boundedness of $M_p$ on the weighted Morrey space $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X),$ for $1<p<q.$.}}
elements.sent-2411-063: If $1<p<\infty, 0<\kappa<1$ and $\omega \in A_p(X),$ then $M_p$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X),$ for $p<q<\infty.$.}}
elements.sent-2411-064: To prove this result, we will use the fact that $M_p(f)(t) = (M(|f|^p)(t))^{\frac{1 {p }$.}}
elements.sent-2411-065: Now using the Theorem \ref{bhl , we obtain \begin{equation}\|M_p(f)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}=\left(\frac{1}{\omega(B)^\kappa}\int_B |M_p(f)(t)|^q\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} = \left(\frac{1}{\omega(B)^\kappa}\int_B |M(|f|^p)(t)|^{\frac{q}{p}}\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} \leq \|M(|f|^p)\|_{L_{\omega}^{\frac{q}{p},\kappa}(X)}^{1/p} \lesssim \||f|^p\|_{L_{\omega}^{\frac{q}{p},\kappa}(X)}^{1/p} = \|f\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}\end{equation} Hence we obtain the boundeness of $M_p$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X),$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-066: The following boundedness result easily follows from \cite{gvw [Theorem 1.1], taking $\lambda_1= \lambda_2$.}

elements.sent-2411-068: Using $M_p(f)(t) = (M(|f|^p)(t))^{\frac{1 {p }$ and the lemma above, we obtain similar boundedness result for the commutator $[b, M_p]$ when $b\in BMO(X)$ for any $p\in [1,\infty).$.}}
elements.sent-2411-069: The following boundedness charaterization is known for the fractional maximal function on the weighted Morrey spaces.
elements.sent-2411-070: Note that, the maximal commutator of fractional maximal function $M_\gamma$ with the locally integrable function $b$ is given by \begin{equation}M_{\gamma,b} := \sup_{B \ni x} \frac{1}{\mu(B)^{1-\gamma}}\int_B |b(x)-b(y)||f(y)|\,d\mu(y),\end{equation} where the supremum is taken over all balls $B\subset X$.

elements.sent-2411-082: Now observe that \begin{equation}\label{eq1} \left(\int_{B}|b(t)M_p(f)(t)-M_p(bf)(t)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} \leq \|[M_p, b](f)\|_{L^q(X,\omega)}\end{equation} Since for $t\in B$, we have \begin{equation}\label{eq2} M_p(bf)(t) = \frac{1}{\omega(B)^{\kappa/q}}M_p(bh)(t) \quad and \quad M_p(f)(t) = \frac{1}{\omega(B)^{\kappa/q}}M_p(h)(t)\end{equation} Hence using \ref{eq2 and plugging this in \ref{eq1 we get, \begin{equation}\left(\frac{1}{\omega(B)^{\kappa}} \int_{B}|b(t)M_p(h)(t)-M_p(bh)(t)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} \leq \|[M_p, b](f)\|_{L^q(X,\omega)} \lesssim \|f\|_{L^q(X,\omega)} \leq \left(\frac{1}{\omega(B)^\kappa}\int_{B}|h(t)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q}\end{equation} Therefore \begin{equation}\|[M_p, b](h)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)} \lesssim \|h\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}.\end{equation}.}}

elements.sent-2411-100: To prove the sufficiency, that is if $b\in BMO(X)$, to show the boundedness of $C_b$ on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X)$ we will use the Lemma \ref{bhl and the following inequality (given in \cite{mgkm ) \begin{equation}C_b(f)(x) \leq C\|b\|_{BMO(X)}M^2(f)(x)\end{equation} Therefore we obtain, \begin{equation}\|C_b(f)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}\leq C \|b\|_{BMO(X)} \|M^2(f)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)} \leq C \|b\|_{BMO(X)}\|f\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}.\end{equation}.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-101: To prove the necessity, let us assume $C_b$ is bounded on $L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X)$, we want to show that $b\in BMO(X).$ Observe \begin{equation}\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\inf_{c\in R}\int_B|b(t)-c|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(B)}\inf_{x\in B}\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(B)^2}\int_B\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(t)d\mu(x) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(B)^2}\int_B\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|\omega(t)^{\frac{1}{p}}\omega(t)^{\frac{-1}{p}}d\mu(t)d\mu(x) \leq \left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(x)\right)^p\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B\omega(t)^{\frac{-p'}{p}}d\mu(t)\right)^{1/p'} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{-1}{p}}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(x)\right)^p\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p}(\omega(B))^{\frac{-1}{p}}\left(\int_B\left(\frac{1}{\mu(B)}\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(x)\right)^p\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p}(\omega(B))^{\frac{-1}{p}}\left(\int_B|C_b(\chi_B)|^p\omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p} (\omega(B))^{\frac{\kappa-1}{p}} \|C_b(\chi_B)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p} (\omega(B))^{\frac{\kappa-1}{p}} \|\chi_B\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)} \lesssim [\omega]_{A_p}^{1/p} (\omega(B))^{\frac{\kappa-1}{p}}(\omega(B))^{\frac{1-\kappa}{q}} \leq C.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-102: Here we used that $\frac{1 {\mu(B)}\int_B|b(t)-b(x)|d\mu(x) \leq C_b(\chi_B)(t)$ in the inequalities above.}

elements.sent-2411-110: For any quasi-linear operator $T$ and general BMO function $b$ we have the following \begin{equation}\label{eqine} |[T,b](f)-[T,|b|](f)|\leq 2(b^- T(f) + T(b^- f))\end{equation} As $M^{\sharp}$ is a quasi-linear operator, \eqref{eqine holds true.}

elements.sent-2411-113: Let us fix a ball $Q$ and assume $\chi_Q \in L_{\omega ^{q,\kappa (X)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-114: We know that $M^{\sharp (\chi_Q) = \frac{1 {2}$, now using $(ii)$, we have \begin{equation}\|[M^{\sharp}, b](\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)} \leq C\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}\end{equation} which is same as \begin{equation}\left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)^{\kappa}} \int_{Q}|b(t)M^{\sharp}(\chi_Q)(t)-M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} \leq C\omega(Q)^{\frac{1-\kappa}{q}} \left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)} \int_{Q}|b(t)-2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} \leq \tilde{C}\end{equation} Hence we obtain $(iii)$ \begin{equation}\sup_{Q}\frac{\|(b - 2M^{\sharp}(b))\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}}=\sup_Q \left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)} \int_{Q}|b(t)-2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)|^q \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{1/q} < \infty.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-116: The proof here is similar to proof of Theorem \ref{thm main1 .}
elements.sent-2411-117: Consider for a ball $Q$, a ball $\tilde{Q}$ containing $Q$ such that $\mu(\tilde{Q ) = 2\mu(Q)$.}
elements.sent-2411-118: Suppose that $x\in Q$, then we have \begin{equation}\frac{1}{2\mu(Q)} \int_{Q}|b(t)-\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{4}|b_Q| = \frac{1}{2\mu(Q)} \left( \int_{Q}|b(t)-\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{2} \mu(\tilde{Q} \setminus Q)|b_Q|\right) =\frac{1}{\mu(\tilde{Q})}\int_{Q}|b\chi_Q(t)-(b\chi_Q)_{\tilde{Q}}|d\mu(t)+ \int_{\tilde{Q}\setminus Q}|b\chi_Q(t)-(b\chi_Q)_{\tilde{Q}}|d\mu(t) =\frac{1}{\mu(\tilde{Q})}\int_{\tilde{Q}}|b\chi_Q(t)-(b\chi_Q)_{\tilde{Q}}|d\mu(t) \leq M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(x).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-120: Now, observe that \begin{equation}|b_Q| \leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)} \int_{Q}|b(t)-\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q |\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t) = \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\left( \int_{Q}|b(t)-\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{2}|b_Q|\right),\end{equation} So, we get \begin{equation}|b_Q|\leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)} \int_{Q}|b(t)-\frac{1}{2}b_Q|d\mu(t)+\frac{1}{2}|b_Q| \leq 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(x) \quad \forall x\in Q.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-122: Let us consider $E=\{x\in Q: b(x)\leq b_Q\},$ then using the same strategy as used in the proof of the Theorem \ref{thm main1 , we have \begin{equation}\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q|b(t) - b_{Q}|d\mu(t) = \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_E (b_{Q}-b(t))d\mu(t) \leq \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_E|2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)-b(t)|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_E|2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)-b(t)|\omega(t)^{\frac{1}{q}}\omega(t)^{\frac{-1}{q}}d\mu(t) \leq 2\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_B|b(t) - 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)|d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \times \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_B \omega(t)^{\frac{-q'}{q}}d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} \lesssim 2 [\omega]_{A_q}^{1/q}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_B|b(t) - 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)|d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \times \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_B \omega(t)d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{-1}{q}} \lesssim 2 [\omega]_{A_q}^{1/q}\left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)}\int_B|b(t) - 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)|d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \leq 2 [\omega]_{A_q}^{1/q} \sup_{Q}\frac{\|(b - 2M^{\sharp}(b))\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega}^{q,\kappa}(X)}} < \infty.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-123: To show that b is bounded, consider \begin{equation}|b_Q| - b^+(t) + b^-(t) \leq 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t) - b(t)\end{equation} Now averaging over $Q$, we have \begin{equation}|b_Q| - \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q b^+(t) d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q b^-(t)d\mu(t) = \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q ( |b_Q| - b^+(t) + b^-(t))d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q |2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t) - b(t)|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q |2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t) - b(t)|\omega(t)^{\frac{1}{q}}\omega(t)^{\frac{-1}{q}}d\mu(t) \lesssim \left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)}\int_B|b(t) - 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)|d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \leq C\end{equation} Let $\mu(Q) \rightarrow 0$ with $t\in Q,$ then using the Lebesgue differentiation theorem we obtain the following \begin{equation}2b^-(t) = |b(t)|- b^+(t) + b^-(t) \leq C.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-056: \begin{equation}|b_Q| - \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q b^+(t) d\mu(t) + \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q b^-(t)d\mu(t) = \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q ( |b_Q| - b^+(t) + b^-(t))d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q |2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t) - b(t)|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q |2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t) - b(t)|\omega(t)^{\frac{1}{q}}\omega(t)^{\frac{-1}{q}}d\mu(t) \lesssim \left(\frac{1}{\omega(Q)}\int_B|b(t) - 2M^{\sharp}(b\chi_Q)(t)|^q \omega(t)|d\mu(t)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \leq C\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-124: To show the equivalence, we will first show $(i) \implies (ii).$ Assume $b\in BMO(X)$ and $b^- \in L^{\infty (X).$ Now using the equation \ref{relmaxfrac and Lemma \ref{lemfrac2 , Lemma \ref{prop3 , we obtain the following for any $f\in L_{loc ^1(X)$ \begin{equation}\| [b, M_{\gamma}]f\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)} \leq \|M_{b,\gamma}(f)(x) + 2b^-(x)M_{\gamma}f(x)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)} \leq \|M_{b,\gamma}(f)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)} + \|2b^-(x)M_{\gamma}f(x)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)} \leq \|b\|_{BMO(X)} \|f\|_{L_{(\omega^p,\omega^q)}^{p,\kappa}(X)} + \|b^-\|_{L^{\infty}(X)} \|f\|_{L_{(\omega^p,\omega^q)}^{p,\kappa}(X)} \lesssim \|f\|_{L_{(\omega^p, \omega^q)}^{p,\kappa}(X)}.\end{equation}.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-125: This gives us $(ii).$ Now let us show $(ii) \implies (iii)$.

elements.sent-2411-127: We will now show there is a constant $C$ such that \eqref{eqfrac holds.}
elements.sent-2411-128: Note that \begin{equation}\|(b - \mu(Q)^{-\gamma}M_{\gamma,Q}(b)\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)} = \left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}} \int_Q|b(x)-\mu(Q)^{-\gamma}M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} = \frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left( \frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q|b(x)\mu(Q)^{\gamma}-M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}\end{equation} For any given ball $Q$ and given any $x\in Q$, we can easily show \begin{equation}M_{\gamma}(b\chi_Q)(x) = M_{\gamma, Q}(b)(x) \quad and \quad M_{\gamma}(\chi_Q)(x) = \mu(Q)^{\gamma}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-130: Hence, using $(ii)$ and the equation \eqref{eqcommu , it follows that \begin{equation}\frac{\|(b(x) - \mu(Q)^{-\gamma}M_{\gamma,Q}(b)\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left( \frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q|b(x)\mu(Q)^{\gamma}-M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} =\frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}} \int_Q|[b, M_{\gamma}](\chi_Q)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\|[b, M_{\gamma}](\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} \lesssim \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{(\omega^p,\omega^q)}^{p,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\kappa}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\frac{1}{p}-\frac{1}{q}}}\left(\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\frac{1} {\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} \lesssim \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}} \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^{-q'}(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} [w^q]_{A_q}^{\frac{1}{q}} = [w]_{A_{q,p}} [w^q]_{A_q}^{\frac{1}{q}} < \infty.\end{equation}.}
elements.formula-2411-062: \begin{equation}\frac{\|(b(x) - \mu(Q)^{-\gamma}M_{\gamma,Q}(b)\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left( \frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q|b(x)\mu(Q)^{\gamma}-M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} =\frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}} \int_Q|[b, M_{\gamma}](\chi_Q)(x)|^q \omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\|[b, M_{\gamma}](\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} \lesssim \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{(\omega^p,\omega^q)}^{p,\kappa}(X)}}{\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\kappa}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\gamma}}\left(\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\frac{1}{\mu(Q)^{\frac{1}{p}-\frac{1}{q}}}\left(\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} = \frac{\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}}}{\left(\frac{1} {\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}} \lesssim \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^p(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{p}} \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^{-q'}(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} [w^q]_{A_q}^{\frac{1}{q}} = [w]_{A_{q,p}} [w^q]_{A_q}^{\frac{1}{q}} < \infty.\end{equation}

elements.sent-2411-136: Hence we have \begin{equation}\|b\|_{BMO(X)} =\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q|b(x)-b_Q|d\mu(x) \leq \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_E|b(x)-\mu^{-\gamma}(Q)M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|d\mu(t) \leq \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q|b(x)-\mu^{-\gamma}(Q)M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|d\mu(t) = \frac{2}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q|b(x)-\mu^{-\gamma}(Q)M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|\omega(x)\omega^{-1}(x)d\mu(x) \leq 2 \left(\frac{\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}{\mu(Q)}\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}} \int_Q|b(x)-\mu^{-\gamma}(Q)M_{\gamma,Q}(b)(x)|^q\omega^q(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}} \left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q \omega^{-q'}(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} = 2\left(\frac{\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa }{p}}{\mu(Q)^{\frac{1}{q}}}\right)\|(b(x) - \mu(Q)^{-\gamma}M_{\gamma,Q}(b)\chi_Q)\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q \omega^{-q'}(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} \lesssim 2\left(\frac{\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa }{p}}{\mu(Q)^{\frac{1}{q}}}\right)\|\chi_Q\|_{L_{\omega^q}^{q,\frac{\kappa q}{p}}(X)}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q \omega^{-q'}(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} = 2\left( \frac{\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa }{p}}{\mu(Q)^{\frac{1}{q}}}\right)\left(\frac{1}{(\omega^q(Q))^\frac{\kappa q}{p}}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q \omega^{-q'}(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} = 2\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q\omega^q(x) d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q}}\left(\frac{1}{\mu(Q)}\int_Q \omega^{-q'}(x)d\mu(x)\right)^{\frac{1}{q'}} = 2[w^q]_{A_q}^{\frac{1}{q}} < \infty.\end{equation}.
